title: I Am Where I Am. There's No Behind.
subtitle: An affirmation for the time-anxiety that runs all day
excerpt: A short affirmation for the persistent sense of being behind. The statement names the position clearly and rejects the frame of deficit that runs alongside it.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the feeling of being behind has been running for a while. Good mid-day or when the gap between what was planned and what has happened feels like failure.

content.text: A one-minute affirmation for the behind-feeling. The feeling that you are behind implies a schedule you were supposed to be keeping. The affirmation names where you actually are and drops the implied deficit.
New to affirmations as a practice? Read
I am where I am. There is no behind. The work I do today is the work I do today.
Say it when the behind-feeling is running. Say it at the start of the day if it starts early. Say it again at the end if it is still there.
